title: Gaertneria fruticosa
intro: Gaertneria fruticosa, also known as the "Indian Tinospora", is a type of climbing vine native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Gaertneria fruticosa include alkaloids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Gaertneria fruticosa include decoctions, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
